steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Move the constant and complete the square.
$$
x^2-4x-29=0
$$
$$
x^2-4x=29
$$
$$
x^2-4x+4=33
$$

steps.title: Solve the square equation
steps.content:
Rewrite the left side as a square, then take square roots.
$$
(x-2)^2=33
$$
$$
x=2\pm\sqrt{33}
$$

steps.title: Match the radical value
steps.content: One solution has the form $$ 2+\sqrt{k} $$, so $k=33$. The answer is B.
